sections.paragraph:
ἐξομοιόω aor. ptc. ἐξομοιωθέντες (Just., D. 44, 2; 45, 4) ‘to make just like or similar’ (Hdt. et al.; Sextus 381). Pass. in act. sense become just like/similar, resemble fully (Trag. et al.; 2 Macc 4:16; Philo, Aet. M. 44; Just.); τινί someone (Epict. 1, 2, 18; 2, 14, 12; Socrat., Ep. 6, 4; Just., D. s. above, also 122, 2) τέλεον αὐτοῖς ἐξομοιοῦσθε finally you become quite like them Dg 2:5.—DELG s.v. ὁμός.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

act. act. = active

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 351
